revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster |native_name =新田原古墳群 |alternate_name = |image =Minakamizuka_Kofun（Nyutabaru_No.56).jpg |alt = |caption = Nyūtabaru Kofun No.56 | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates = {{coord|32|04|47|N|131|25|50|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}} | location = [[Shintomi, Miyazaki|Shintomi]], [[Saito, Miyazaki]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis |part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = | built =c. 4th to 6th century AD |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|04|47|N|131|25|50|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster'''|新田原古墳群| Nyūtabaru kofungun}} is the name for four groups of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Nitta neighborhood of the town of [[Shintomi, Miyazaki|Shintomi]] and the Umatsu neighborhood of the city of [[Saito, Miyazaki|Saito]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217899|title= 新田原古墳群|trans-title= Nyūtabaru Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=November 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Background== The Nyūtabaru Plateau is at an elevation of about 70 meters on the left bank of the Hitotsuse River that flows southeast from Hitotsuse Dam through Saito City and into the [[Hyūga Sea]]. The plateau contains 207 known burial mounds of various sizes and types. These are divided into four groups based on their distribution and construction period, and from the east to west are name the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster, Ishifune Kofun Cluster, Yamanobo Kofun Cluster, and Gionbara Kofun Cluster. The Gionbara group, where the ''kofun'' are most concentrated, consists of 139 {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, 14 keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, one square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus, and one tumulus of unknown shape. One of the ''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]'', the Mukadezuka Kofun (Nyūtabaru No.58) has a length of 80 meters, and had one 1000 ''[[haniwa]]'', and of unique designs not found in other burial mounds. It is thought to have been built in the 6th century (late Kofun period). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The area is approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Hyūga-Shintomi Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mk-heritage/burialmounds/summary_nyutabaru.html Miyazaki Prefecture hone page]{{in lang|ja}}

The plateau contains 207 known Kofuns of various sizes and types. These are divided into four groups based on their distribution and construction period, and from the east to west are name the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster, Ishifune Kofun Cluster, Yamanobo Kofun Cluster, and Gionbara Kofun Cluster. The Gionbara group, where the ''kofun'' are most concentrated, consists of 139 {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, 14 keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, one square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus, and one tumulus of unknown shape. It is thought to have been built in the 6th century (late Kofun period). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The {{Nihongo|'''Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster'''|新田原古墳群| Nyūtabaru kofungun}} is the name for four groups of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Nitta neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Shintomi, Miyazaki|Shintomi]] and the Umatsu neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217899|title= 新田原古墳群|trans-title= Nyūtabaru Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=November 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Background== The Nyūtabaru Plateau is at an elevation of about 70 meters on the left bank of the Hitotsuse River that flows southeast from Hitotsuse Dam through Saito City and into the [[:en:Hyūga Sea|Hyūga Sea]]. The plateau contains 207 known Kofuns of various sizes and types. These are divided into four groups based on their distribution and construction period, and from the east to west are name the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster, Ishifune Kofun Cluster, Yamanobo Kofun Cluster, and Gionbara Kofun Cluster. The Gionbara group, where the ''kofun'' are most concentrated, consists of 139 {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, 14 keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, one square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus, and one tumulus of unknown shape. One of the ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]'', the Mukadezuka Kofun (Nyūtabaru No.58) has a length of 80 meters, and had one 1000 ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', and of unique designs not found in other Kofuns. It is thought to have been built in the 6th century (late Kofun period). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]][[en:Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]][[ja:新田原古墳群]][[zh:新田原古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22119904]] Wikidata name: Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster |native_name =新田原古墳群 |alternate_name = |image =Minakamizuka_Kofun（Nyutabaru_No.56).jpg; File:Nyutabaru Kofungun.jpg|alt = |caption = Nyūtabaru Kofun No.56 | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates ={{coord|32|04|47|N|131|25|50|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}}; {{Coord|32.102444|N|131.427583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| location =[[:en:Shintomi, Miyazaki|Shintomi]], [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Saito|WD=Q850479}}; {{ill|Shintomi|WD=Q1206753}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis | part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = | built =c. 4th to 6th century AD |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|04|47|N|131|25|50|E}}}}|Historic Site= {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}} }}
The {{Nihongo|'''Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster'''|新田原古墳群| Nyūtabaru kofungun}} is the name for four groups of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Nitta neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Shintomi, Miyazaki|Shintomi]] and the Umatsu neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217899|title= 新田原古墳群|trans-title= Nyūtabaru Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=November 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Background== The Nyūtabaru Plateau is at an elevation of about 70 meters on the left bank of the Hitotsuse River that flows southeast from Hitotsuse Dam through Saito City and into the [[:en:Hyūga Sea|Hyūga Sea]]. The plateau contains 207 known Kofuns of various sizes and types. These are divided into four groups based on their distribution and construction period, and from the east to west are name the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster, Ishifune Kofun Cluster, Yamanobo Kofun Cluster, and Gionbara Kofun Cluster. The Gionbara group, where the ''kofun'' are most concentrated, consists of 139 {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, 14 keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, one square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus, and one tumulus of unknown shape. One of the ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]'', the Mukadezuka Kofun (Nyūtabaru No.58) has a length of 80 meters, and had one 1000 ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', and of unique designs not found in other Kofuns. It is thought to have been built in the 6th century (late Kofun period). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The area is approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Hyūga-Shintomi Station|Hyūga-Shintomi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]][[en:Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]][[ja:新田原古墳群]][[zh:新田原古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22119904]] Wikidata name: Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster |native_name =新田原古墳群 |alternate_name = |image =Minakamizuka_Kofun（Nyutabaru_No.56).jpg; File:Nyutabaru Kofungun.jpg|alt = |caption = Nyūtabaru Kofun No.56 | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates ={{coord|32|04|47|N|131|25|50|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}}; {{Coord|32.102444|N|131.427583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| location =[[:en:Shintomi, Miyazaki|Shintomi]], [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Saito|WD=Q850479}}; {{ill|Shintomi|WD=Q1206753}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis | part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = | built =c. 4th to 6th century AD |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|04|47|N|131|25|50|E}}}}|Historic Site= {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}} }}
The {{Nihongo|'''Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster'''|新田原古墳群| Nyūtabaru kofungun}} is the name for four groups of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Nitta neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Shintomi, Miyazaki|Shintomi]] and the Umatsu neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217899|title= 新田原古墳群|trans-title= Nyūtabaru Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=November 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Background== The Nyūtabaru Plateau is at an elevation of about 70 meters on the left bank of the Hitotsuse River that flows southeast from Hitotsuse Dam through Saito City and into the [[:en:Hyūga Sea|Hyūga Sea]]. The plateau contains 207 known Kofuns of various sizes and types. These are divided into four groups based on their distribution and construction period, and from the east to west are name the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster, Ishifune Kofun Cluster, Yamanobo Kofun Cluster, and Gionbara Kofun Cluster. The Gionbara group, where the ''kofun'' are most concentrated, consists of 139 {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, 14 keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, one square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus, and one tumulus of unknown shape. One of the ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]'', the Mukadezuka Kofun (Nyūtabaru No.58) has a length of 80 meters, and had one 1000 ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', and of unique designs not found in other Kofuns. It is thought to have been built in the 6th century (late Kofun period). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The area is approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Hyūga-Shintomi Station|Hyūga-Shintomi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mk-heritage/burialmounds/summary_nyutabaru.html Miyazaki Prefecture hone page]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]][[en:Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]][[ja:新田原古墳群]][[zh:新田原古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]][[en:Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]][[ja:新田原古墳群]][[zh:新田原古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]][[en:Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]][[ja:新田原古墳群]][[zh:新田原古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22119904]] Wikidata name: Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster |native_name =新田原古墳群 |alternate_name = |image =Minakamizuka_Kofun（Nyutabaru_No.56).jpg; File:Nyutabaru Kofungun.jpg|alt = |caption = Nyūtabaru Kofun No.56 | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates ={{coord|32|04|47|N|131|25|50|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}}; {{Coord|32.102444|N|131.427583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| location =[[:en:Shintomi, Miyazaki|Shintomi]], [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Saito|WD=Q850479}}; {{ill|Shintomi|WD=Q1206753}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis | part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = | built =c. 4th to 6th century AD |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|04|47|N|131|25|50|E}}}}|Historic Site= {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}} }}
The {{Nihongo|'''Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster'''|新田原古墳群| Nyūtabaru kofungun}} is the name for four groups of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Nitta neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Shintomi, Miyazaki|Shintomi]] and the Umatsu neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217899|title= 新田原古墳群|trans-title= Nyūtabaru Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=November 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Background== The Nyūtabaru Plateau is at an elevation of about 70 meters on the left bank of the Hitotsuse River that flows southeast from Hitotsuse Dam through Saito City and into the [[:en:Hyūga Sea|Hyūga Sea]]. The plateau contains 207 known Kofuns of various sizes and types. These are divided into four groups based on their distribution and construction period, and from the east to west are name the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster, Ishifune Kofun Cluster, Yamanobo Kofun Cluster, and Gionbara Kofun Cluster. The Gionbara group, where the ''kofun'' are most concentrated, consists of 139 {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, 14 keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, one square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus, and one tumulus of unknown shape. One of the ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]'', the Mukadezuka Kofun (Nyūtabaru No.58) has a length of 80 meters, and had one 1000 ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', and of unique designs not found in other Kofuns. It is thought to have been built in the 6th century (late Kofun period). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The area is approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Hyūga-Shintomi Station|Hyūga-Shintomi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]

revision.comment: Bot: categorize pages with unclosed brackets
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]][[en:Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]][[ja:新田原古墳群]][[zh:新田原古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]][[en:Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]][[ja:新田原古墳群]][[zh:新田原古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]][[en:Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]][[ja:新田原古墳群]][[zh:新田原古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22119904]] Wikidata name: Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster |native_name =新田原古墳群 |alternate_name = |image =Minakamizuka_Kofun（Nyutabaru_No.56).jpg; File:Nyutabaru Kofungun.jpg|alt = |caption = Nyūtabaru Kofun No.56 | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates ={{coord|32|04|47|N|131|25|50|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}}; {{Coord|32.102444|N|131.427583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| location =[[:en:Shintomi, Miyazaki|Shintomi]], [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Saito|WD=Q850479}}; {{ill|Shintomi|WD=Q1206753}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis | part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = | built =c. 4th to 6th century AD |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|04|47|N|131|25|50|E}}}}|Historic Site= {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}} }}
The {{Nihongo|'''Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster'''|新田原古墳群| Nyūtabaru kofungun}} is the name for four groups of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Nitta neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Shintomi, Miyazaki|Shintomi]] and the Umatsu neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217899|title= 新田原古墳群|trans-title= Nyūtabaru Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=November 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Background== The Nyūtabaru Plateau is at an elevation of about 70 meters on the left bank of the Hitotsuse River that flows southeast from Hitotsuse Dam through Saito City and into the [[:en:Hyūga Sea|Hyūga Sea]]. The plateau contains 207 known Kofuns of various sizes and types. These are divided into four groups based on their distribution and construction period, and from the east to west are name the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster, Ishifune Kofun Cluster, Yamanobo Kofun Cluster, and Gionbara Kofun Cluster. The Gionbara group, where the ''kofun'' are most concentrated, consists of 139 {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, 14 keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, one square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus, and one tumulus of unknown shape. One of the ''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]'', the Mukadezuka Kofun (Nyūtabaru No.58) has a length of 80 meters, and had one 1000 ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', and of unique designs not found in other Kofuns. It is thought to have been built in the 6th century (late Kofun period). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The area is approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Hyūga-Shintomi Station|Hyūga-Shintomi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mk-heritage/burialmounds/summary_nyutabaru.html Miyazaki Prefecture hone page]{{in lang|ja}}

The plateau contains 207 known Kofuns of various sizes and types. These are divided into four groups based on their distribution and construction period, and from the east to west are name the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster, Ishifune Kofun Cluster, Yamanobo Kofun Cluster, and Gionbara Kofun Cluster. The Gionbara group, where the ''kofun'' are most concentrated, consists of 139 {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, 14 keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, one square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus, and one tumulus of unknown shape. One of the ''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}'', the Mukadezuka Kofun (Nyūtabaru No.58) has a length of 80 meters, and had one 1000 ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', and of unique designs not found in other Kofuns. It is thought to have been built in the 6th century (late Kofun period). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The area is approximately 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Hyūga-Shintomi Station|en|Hyūga-Shintomi Station|WD=Q5357437|lt=Hyūga-Shintomi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|WD=Q6573277|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)}}

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster |native_name =新田原古墳群 |alternate_name = |image =Minakamizuka_Kofun（Nyutabaru_No.56).jpg; File:Nyutabaru Kofungun.jpg|alt = |caption = Nyūtabaru Kofun No.56 | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates ={{coord|32|04|47|N|131|25|50|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}}; {{Coord|32.102444|N|131.427583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| location ={{ill|Shintomi, Miyazaki|en|Shintomi, Miyazaki|WD=Q1206753|lt=Shintomi}}, {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|WD=Q850479|lt=Saito, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}; {{ill|Saito|WD=Q850479}}; {{ill|Shintomi|WD=Q1206753}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis | part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = | built =c. 4th to 6th century AD |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|04|47|N|131|25|50|E}}}}|Historic Site= {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}} }}
The {{Nihongo|'''Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster'''|新田原古墳群| Nyūtabaru kofungun}} is the name for four groups of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Nitta neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Shintomi, Miyazaki|en|Shintomi, Miyazaki|WD=Q1206753|lt=Shintomi}} and the Umatsu neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|WD=Q850479|lt=Saito}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}} in [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217899|title= 新田原古墳群|trans-title= Nyūtabaru Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=November 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Background== The Nyūtabaru Plateau is at an elevation of about 70 meters on the left bank of the Hitotsuse River that flows southeast from Hitotsuse Dam through Saito City and into the {{ill|Hyūga Sea|en|Hyūga Sea|WD=Q7440098|lt=Hyūga Sea}}. The plateau contains 207 known Kofuns of various sizes and types. These are divided into four groups based on their distribution and construction period, and from the east to west are name the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster, Ishifune Kofun Cluster, Yamanobo Kofun Cluster, and Gionbara Kofun Cluster. The Gionbara group, where the ''kofun'' are most concentrated, consists of 139 {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, 14 keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, one square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus, and one tumulus of unknown shape. One of the ''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}'', the Mukadezuka Kofun (Nyūtabaru No.58) has a length of 80 meters, and had one 1000 ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', and of unique designs not found in other Kofuns. It is thought to have been built in the 6th century (late Kofun period). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The area is approximately 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Hyūga-Shintomi Station|en|Hyūga-Shintomi Station|WD=Q5357437|lt=Hyūga-Shintomi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|WD=Q6573277|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)}}
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mk-heritage/burialmounds/summary_nyutabaru.html Miyazaki Prefecture hone page]{{in lang|ja}}

The plateau contains 207 known Kofuns of various sizes and types. These are divided into four groups based on their distribution and construction period, and from the east to west are name the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster, Ishifune Kofun Cluster, Yamanobo Kofun Cluster, and Gionbara Kofun Cluster. The Gionbara group, where the ''kofun'' are most concentrated, consists of 139 {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, 14 keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, one square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus, and one tumulus of unknown shape. One of the ''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}'', the Mukadezuka Kofun (Nyūtabaru No.58) has a length of 80 meters, and had one 1000 ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', and of unique designs not found in other Kofuns. It is thought to have been built in the 6th century (late Kofun period). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The area is approximately 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Hyūga-Shintomi Station|en|Hyūga-Shintomi Station|WD=Q5357437|lt=Hyūga-Shintomi Station|1=Hyūga-Shintomi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{Nihongo|'''Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster'''|新田原古墳群| Nyūtabaru kofungun}} is the name for four groups of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Nitta neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Shintomi, Miyazaki|en|Shintomi, Miyazaki|WD=Q1206753|lt=Shintomi|1=Shintomi, Miyazaki}} and the Umatsu neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|WD=Q850479|lt=Saito|1=Saito, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} in [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217899|title= 新田原古墳群|trans-title= Nyūtabaru Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=November 1, 2023}}</ref>

The plateau contains 207 known Kofuns of various sizes and types. These are divided into four groups based on their distribution and construction period, and from the east to west are name the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster, Ishifune Kofun Cluster, Yamanobo Kofun Cluster, and Gionbara Kofun Cluster. The Gionbara group, where the ''kofun'' are most concentrated, consists of 139 {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, 14 keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, one square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus, and one tumulus of unknown shape. One of the ''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}'', the Mukadezuka Kofun (Nyūtabaru No.58) has a length of 80 meters, and had one 1000 ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', and of unique designs not found in other Kofuns. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]][[en:Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]][[ja:新田原古墳群]][[zh:新田原古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]][[en:Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]][[ja:新田原古墳群]][[zh:新田原古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster]][[ja:新田原古墳群]][[zh:新田原古墳群]]
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster |native_name =新田原古墳群 |alternate_name = |image =Minakamizuka_Kofun（Nyutabaru_No.56).jpg; File:Nyutabaru Kofungun.jpg|alt = |caption = Nyūtabaru Kofun No.56 | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates ={{coord|32|04|47|N|131|25|50|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}}; {{Coord|32.102444|N|131.427583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| location ={{ill|Shintomi, Miyazaki|en|Shintomi, Miyazaki|WD=Q1206753|lt=Shintomi|1=Shintomi, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|WD=Q850479|lt=Saito, Miyazaki|1=Saito, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Saito|WD=Q850479|1=Saito, Miyazaki}}; {{ill|Shintomi|WD=Q1206753|1=Shintomi, Miyazaki}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis | part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = | built =c. 4th to 6th century AD |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|04|47|N|131|25|50|E}}}}|Historic Site= {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}} }}
The {{Nihongo|'''Nyūtabaru Kofun Cluster'''|新田原古墳群| Nyūtabaru kofungun}} is the name for four groups of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Nitta neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Shintomi, Miyazaki|en|Shintomi, Miyazaki|WD=Q1206753|lt=Shintomi|1=Shintomi, Miyazaki}} and the Umatsu neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|WD=Q850479|lt=Saito|1=Saito, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} in [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1944. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217899|title= 新田原古墳群|trans-title= Nyūtabaru Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=November 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Background== The Nyūtabaru Plateau is at an elevation of about 70 meters on the left bank of the Hitotsuse River that flows southeast from Hitotsuse Dam through Saito City and into the {{ill|Hyūga Sea|en|Hyūga Sea|WD=Q7440098|lt=Hyūga Sea|1=Hyūga Sea}}. The plateau contains 207 known Kofuns of various sizes and types. These are divided into four groups based on their distribution and construction period, and from the east to west are name the Tsukahara Kofun Cluster, Ishifune Kofun Cluster, Yamanobo Kofun Cluster, and Gionbara Kofun Cluster. The Gionbara group, where the ''kofun'' are most concentrated, consists of 139 {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumuli, 14 keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, one square {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus, and one tumulus of unknown shape. One of the ''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}'', the Mukadezuka Kofun (Nyūtabaru No.58) has a length of 80 meters, and had one 1000 ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', and of unique designs not found in other Kofuns. It is thought to have been built in the 6th century (late Kofun period). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The area is approximately 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Hyūga-Shintomi Station|en|Hyūga-Shintomi Station|WD=Q5357437|lt=Hyūga-Shintomi Station|1=Hyūga-Shintomi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|WD=Q6573277|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)}}
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mk-heritage/burialmounds/summary_nyutabaru.html Miyazaki Prefecture hone page]{{in lang|ja}}
